title: Cheirolophus intybaceus

Cheirolophus intybaceus is a type of shrub and has a distinct appearance with its leaves and flowers. The plant is also known by the name Sardinian tree daisy. It is a rare and endangered species, making conservation efforts important for its survival.

intro: Cheirolophus intybaceus, also known as the Bougainville Island silver tree or the Silver tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Cheirolophus intybaceus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
